{"id":"project-LICHEN-4as2","title":"announce: AnnounceMessage dataclass allows mutation despite immutable-ish comment","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/messages.py:78-86, scheduler.py:179

The AnnounceMessage is a plain @dataclass without frozen=True, making all fields mutable:

```python
@dataclass
class AnnounceMessage:
    originator_iid: bytes  # Mutable
    pubkey: bytes          # Mutable
    ...
```

The comment in scheduler.py says 'AnnounceMessage is immutable-ish' but this is not enforced:

```python
# Why create new message with signature: AnnounceMessage is immutable-ish.
return AnnounceMessage(...)
```

This creates a subtle bug risk: if code mutates a message after signature verification, the signature becomes invalid but the object doesn't indicate this.

Example:
```python
msg = AnnounceMessage.from_bytes(data)
# Signature verified
msg.app_data = b'modified'  # Now signature is invalid but msg doesn't know
```

Fix: Add frozen=True to the dataclass decorator:
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AnnounceMessage:
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:27.717363Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:19:27.717396Z"}
